other_observers: Brenda Wright who first noticed the bird was walking with me on our daily bird walk.; Brenda Wright saw the bird in the shrub. She called it to my attention and immediately the bird flew and we both knew it was a cuckoo. As luck would have it the bird flew across the road but landed in a tree close by where we could study the bird.
specific_location: 1/2 mile from our house in Nucla near Ash and 5th Avenue in southeast part of town
description: Cuckoo size or about the size of a Western Scrub-Jay which is a common species in Nucla. Slender bird with a long tail and medium to long bill which was down-curved. The bird was uniform brown for head to tail on the top side. The underneath side was white to off white from bill to undertail coverts. The underneath side of the tail was gray with indistinct markings. The eye was reddish, brown with a buffy, orange eyering. The bill was slim, down curved and completely dark brown.Behaviors: The bird was sitting in a shrub with just its head sticking out. Then if flew accoss the road to a Russian olive tree where is perched for 30 minutes eating an occasional Russian olive berry. Brenda and I watch the bird from all angles. After a few minutes I decided we should get a camera and I walked home 1/2 mile and then drove back with Brenda's camera. Brenda studied the bird while I was gone as it stayed in the same tree. Brenda did get some photos before the bird flew on to another olive tree.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenile
photographer: Photographer: Brenda Wright, Coen Dexter, Coen Dexter, Coen Dexter, Coen Dexter
